title: Payment Terms
text: The Buyer shall pay the Supplier the purchase price as specified in Schedule 1 within thirty (30) days of receipt of a valid invoice accompanied by the relevant delivery documentation. All payments shall be made by electronic transfer to the Supplier's designated bank account. In the event of delayed payment, the Supplier shall be entitled to charge simple interest at the rate of eighteen percent (18%) per annum on the overdue amount from the due date until actual payment. All amounts are exclusive of GST and other applicable taxes which shall be borne by the Buyer. The Buyer shall not withhold payment on account of any disputed claim without the Supplier's written consent.
statutory_reference: Indian Contract Act, 1872 – S.55; MSMED Act, 2006
name: Supply Agreement Payment Terms

legal_basis.act: Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017

source: Sale of Goods Act, 1930; MSMED Act, 2006; IT Act, 1961
dispute_hotspots: Section 194Q TDS non-deduction — both buyer and seller face IT scrutiny
MSME Samadhan portal — supplier files complaint for delayed payment
GST ITC disputes — mismatch between supplier's GSTR-1 and buyer's GSTR-2B
